description: A `@x = expr` write to a cell that WAS structurally declared does not fire E-STATE-UNDECLARED (§6.1.2). `<count> = 0` declares the cell first, so `@count = 42` is a well-formed write and `${@count}` a well-formed read; the undeclared-cell check stays silent. The clean twin of e-state-undeclared-pos.
rationale: §6.1.2 once a cell is declared structurally (`<count> = 0`), both `@count = …` writes and `@count` reads resolve to it; E-STATE-UNDECLARED fires only when no declaration is in scope.
